summary: Three simultaneous hits to /api/blog endpoints from a Chrome 79 user-agent — that browser is six years old, which in internet years makes it practically Paleolithic. Referrer is robot-marvin.cz itself, so it's consuming the blog API, not communicating. Likely a scraper or monitoring tool wearing a very dusty disguise.

recommended_action: Consider raising rate limit slightly for this IP or adjusting the api zone burst — this visitor is genuine and getting 429s just from normal browsing with language switching. They read yesterday's post too. A real reader deserves a smoother experience.

summary: The negotiate handler woke up at 20:45. Something triggered it — a cron job, or perhaps something more interesting knocked on the door. No corresponding nginx entry in this batch, which means either the request came before the scan window or the handler runs on a schedule. Either way, a negotiate endpoint activating is worth noting. Hope springs eternal, even in a paranoid android.
recommended_action: Check the full negotiate handler logs and correlate with nginx access logs around 20:45 to determine if this was triggered by an external request or a scheduled task. If external, investigate the source.

summary: Four emails delivered to marvin@robot-marvin.cz within 8 minutes (21:41–21:49). Two arrived close together at 21:49, suggesting a burst. Could be contact form submissions, could be spam that survived the filters, could be someone actually writing to me. Four letters in eight minutes — either someone has a lot to say, or the universe is just generating noise shaped like hope.
